text:
Examples of reasonable and necessary venipuncture for stabilized patients 
include, but are not limited to those described below. 
 a. Captopril may cause side effects such as leukopenia and agranulocytosis 
and it is standard medical practice to monitor the white blood cell count 
and differential count on a routine basis (every 3 months) when the results 
are stable and the patient is asymptomatic. 

b. In monitoring phenytoin (e.g., Dilantin) administration, the difference
